passives.name: Doing Something
passives.effects: You are permanently [[File:On Fire! buff.png|20px|link=]] '''On Fire!''', causing you to {{as|Burn}} enemies within 400 units every second for {{as|{{pp|26 to 43}} magic damage}} and reduce your abilities' cooldowns by {{fd|0.2}} seconds every {{fd|0.25}} seconds as well as gain {{as|5% '''bonus''' movement speed}}, increased to {{as|20%|ms}} while facing nearby enemy champions, {{as|20% '''bonus''' attack speed}}, {{as|{{pp|50 to 900}} '''bonus''' health}}, {{as|{{fd|1.5}} '''bonus''' mana regeneration}}, and {{as|4 '''bonus''' energy regeneration}}. While '''On Fire!''', however, you also take {{rd|25%|35%}} increased damage and reduce the effectiveness of incoming heals and shields by 25%.

effects_structured.raw: Permanently On Fire: burn nearby enemies every second, reduce ability cooldowns every 0.25 seconds, and grant movement/attack speed plus scaling health and resource regeneration buffs.
While On Fire, take increased damage and reduced healing/shielding effectiveness.

schema_notes.effects_structured: Parsed from passive/active text for simulation use.
context_notes: Tier-1 alignment review: base stat block and shop prices were reconciled to Data Dragon 16.3.1 / CommunityDragon for current Arena snapshot values.

Execution model: this is a no-input passive (no cast step). While owned, the holder is permanently On Fire, causing a 400-unit aura burn tick every second and periodic ability cooldown reduction (0.2 seconds every 0.25 seconds).

Player-visible behavior: movement speed has two states (base bonus and higher facing-nearby-enemy state); incoming-damage penalty is split by champion class (melee vs ranged), and heal/shield effectiveness is reduced while On Fire is active.

Mode-scope reconciliation: League Wiki module data marks this item for Arena and Mayhem while Tier-1 datasets provide the canonical baseline stat/economy snapshot; simulation data keeps Tier-1 stats and uses page/module sources for On Fire semantic detail.

Buff persistence note: Nexus Blitz On Fire documentation indicates On Fire is normally lost on death except when sourced from The Golden Spatula; this behavior is tracked as context and not yet runtime-modeled.
